caption: M2 strict equal-frame non-oracle winners. The map covers rho=0.001..10 and sigma_a=0.05..0.50.
M2 all-non-oracle winners with reference overhead. The map covers rho=0.001..10 and sigma_a=0.05..0.50.
Observed M2 flip-boundary fits; censored and not-reached crossings are excluded from this compact table.
Residual gain error fits show sigma_delta exponents near 2 with R2 above 0.9999 across bases.
Random-basis residual gain error decreases as frame count increases at fixed image size and sigma_delta=0.05.
Fixed-size random-frame fits estimate num_frames exponents near -0.71 to -0.72.
DCT/Fourier/Hadamard concentrate object energy in top coefficients; random and SRHT spread it.
AGC best-window fits are weak for random/SRHT, indicating the current window grid is diagnostic rather than final.
The targeted AGC sweep expands the window grid but many best windows still sit on the lower boundary.
Targeted AGC fits improve over the earlier diagnostic but remain too boundary-sensitive for a final law.
Boundary-aware AGC fits treat lower-grid selections as bounded optima; random bases reach 0.80 interval satisfaction, while Hadamard remains weak.
